human_verification.notes: Reviewed the package for food-manufacturing specificity, authority-source quality, lifecycle-state clarity, traceability and release boundary handling, and validated spec-pack completeness.

specialist_owned_evidence: Boundary is explicit to food manufacturing execution, line readiness, batch and lot evidence, blocked-work escalation, and completion evidence.
Authority sources cover FDA preventive controls, eCFR cGMP and records posture, FDA traceability and recall boundaries, GS1 traceability standards, and SQFI food-manufacturing operating references.

Evaluation scenarios cover normal in-scope execution, missing prerequisites, engineering and supplier boundary rejection, traceability conflicts, quality holds, unsupported completion, conflicting-policy escalation, execution exceptions, and tenant-specific adaptation.

The package is organized under catalog/naics-overlays/food-and-beverage-manufacturing/food-manufacturing-operations-specialist/ so food manufacturing operations remain distinct from adjacent quality, production planning, inventory, procurement, supplier-quality, and logistics lanes.

review_notes: No unreviewed regressions were identified in the local package review.
Boundary enforcement remained strong around final quality release, engineering change approval, supplier approval, recall authority, and safety override authority.

notes: This package is suitable as a validated food manufacturing operations specialist for line readiness, food production execution, blocked-work handling, lot traceability, and completion evidence capture.
Runtime remains specification-based until a separately promoted retained implementation exists.
